understanding.goal_understanding: The goal seemed to be to maneuver selectable piece(s) around a grid within a limited step budget, likely to reach a goal tile/area (e.g., a '+' goal) by using movement and possibly pushing blocks while navigating walls/obstacles.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (up/down/left/right) attempted to shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in that direction; sometimes the move failed with no position change when blocked by an obstacle or boundary. Multiple selectable pieces existed, and control could be switched instantly using CLICK on another selectable’s exact pixel position. Pushing was implied: if a pushable object was directly adjacent in the movement direction, moving into it could push it along with the selected piece, while walls/solid boundaries could prevent movement; the blocked indicators were only hints and not guaranteed outcomes.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | All directions show adjacent wall, but the hint can be misleading; try moving right to test if this wall blocks or allows sliding/teleport behavior.
history.reasoning: All directions show adjacent wall, but the hint can be misleading; try moving right to test if this wall blocks or allows sliding/teleport behavior.

history.summary: Turn 2: CLICK | All directions appear blocked by walls for the current piece; switch control to the other selectable to test for a viable move/path.
history.reasoning: All directions appear blocked by walls for the current piece; switch control to the other selectable to test for a viable move/path.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_UP | This selectable appears fully enclosed by walls on all four sides; test whether any wall is passable by attempting movement.
history.reasoning: This selectable appears fully enclosed by walls on all four sides; test whether any wall is passable by attempting movement.

history.summary: Turn 4: CLICK | All four directions are adjacent to walls and MOVE_UP already had no effect; try switching back to the other selectable to see if it has an escape route.
history.reasoning: All four directions are adjacent to walls and MOVE_UP already had no effect; try switching back to the other selectable to see if it has an escape route.

history.summary: Turn 5: MOVE_RIGHT | All directions appear blocked, but the heuristic can be misleading; probe the only direction that might allow interaction near the other selectable/wall gap by attempting a right move.
history.reasoning: All directions appear blocked, but the heuristic can be misleading; probe the only direction that might allow interaction near the other selectable/wall gap by attempting a right move.

history.summary: Turn 6: MOVE_RIGHT | We’re enclosed by walls on all four sides, but the only potential interaction is to the right where the other selectable sits; try pushing/moving into it to see if pieces can swap or push through.
history.reasoning: We’re enclosed by walls on all four sides, but the only potential interaction is to the right where the other selectable sits; try pushing/moving into it to see if pieces can swap or push through.

history.summary: Turn 7: CLICK | This piece is fully enclosed by walls on all four sides, so switch control to the other selectable to make progress toward the goal.
history.reasoning: This piece is fully enclosed by walls on all four sides, so switch control to the other selectable to make progress toward the goal.

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_RIGHT | Try moving into the adjacent goal/wall area on the right to see if the goal tile is passable despite the wall hint.
history.reasoning: Try moving into the adjacent goal/wall area on the right to see if the goal tile is passable despite the wall hint.

history.summary: Turn 9: MOVE_UP | All directions appear blocked by wall/goal adjacency, but goals are non-collidable so we should probe for any pass-through; try moving up into the goal area.
history.reasoning: All directions appear blocked by wall/goal adjacency, but goals are non-collidable so we should probe for any pass-through; try moving up into the goal area.
